summary: Good soundboard recording (channel quirks aside) of a hot Duke show, highlighted by a smoking Sugaree and one of the best China Cat Sunflower → Know Your Rider transitions.
review: The SBD captures a lively concert with clear overall mix, though listeners should mind the channel swaps (Jerry center, Brent right, Bob left).  The performance is praised for sweet Jerry leads on Candyman and Sugaree, steady Phil bass work, and an especially memorable China/Know Your Rider segue, making it a solid reference show for this era.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars and highlight strong musical moments; recording is consistently described as good with only minor channel‑placement issues, leading to an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear soundboard source but channels are swapped from this point forward (Jerry center, Brent right, Bob left); first song noted as slightly wavy and occasional channel confusion for headphone listeners.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Above‑average first set and decent second set; the concert is remembered as a hot, high‑energy show with good pacing and memorable transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar work is highlighted as sweet and prominent (Candyman, Sugaree) and his stage position change is noted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s bass described as steady, sub‑sonic and reliable; he also helped fans get backstage passes.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s rhythm guitar/vocals are called "good" and his left‑channel placement is mentioned.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard is noted for its right‑channel placement; no specific performance comments.
